records.ety: [L. obviare; ob (see Ob-) + viare to go, fr. via way. See Voyage.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Obviated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Obviating.]

quotes: Not to stir a step to obviate any of a different religion.

defs: To anticipate; to prevent by interception; to remove from the way or path; to make unnecessary; as, to obviate the necessity of going.
quotes: To lay down everything in its full light, so as to obviate all exceptions.
